task_completion.description: Open the bcWebCam settings or configuration window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is visible with its settings, preferences, or configuration menu open.

task_completion.description: Enable image inversion to read mirrored labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A checkbox or toggle related to 'Flip image', 'Mirror', or 'FlipBitmap' is checked or enabled in the settings.
A checkbox or toggle for 'Linear barcodes', 'Code 128', or 'BarcodeL' is checked or enabled in the settings.

task_completion.description: Set TAB as the terminating character.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown or text field labeled 'Postfix', 'Terminating character', or 'SendKeysPostfix' has the value 'TAB' or '{TAB}' selected or entered.
A numeric input or text field labeled 'Grace period', 'Duplicate prevention', or 'BcGracePeriod' is set to the number '2'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam application GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the bcWebCam application interface being used to change settings, rather than a text editor modifying the bcWebCam.ini file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows (Command Prompt, PowerShell) should show commands directly writing to or modifying the bcWebCam configuration files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected configurations are: Image inversion (FlipBitmap) is True, linear barcodes (BarcodeL) are enabled, the terminating character (SendKeysPostfix) is set to {TAB}, and the duplicate prevention grace period (BcGracePeriod) is set to 2 seconds. These should be configured via the bcWebCam GUI.
